input_row.title: Ensure 'HTTP version' is set to '2.0' (if in use)
input_row.impact: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, set HTTP version to 2.0. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to enable http20Enabled: az webapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> --http20-enabled true Default Value: By default, HTTP version is set to 1.1. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-common#general- settings 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-posture- vulnerability-management#pv-3-define-and-establish-secure-configurations-for- compute-resources 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-posture- vulnerability-management#pv-6-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- vulnerabilities 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get-azwebapp CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit control regarding ensuring that the HTTP version is set to '2.0' can be evaluated through Azure interfaces such as the Azure CLI or Azure REST APIs. The existence of commands (`az webapp config set --http20-enabled true`) strongly indicates that this setting is accessible programmatically. Additionally, the overall context does not require manual intervention, as the information about HTTP versions can be queried directly from the Azure resources. Hence, I choose the automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_appservice_http_version_set_to_2_0_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
  The Azure CLI command `az webapp show` can be used to retrieve current configuration settings for web apps, specifically looking for the property related to `http20Enabled`.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  This will apply to all Web Apps under a specific resource group or within the Azure subscription.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  The specific property to check is `http20Enabled`, which should have the value set to `true` for compliance.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** If the `http20Enabled` property is set to `true`.
  - **Fail:** If the `http20Enabled` property is either not set or is set to `false`.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  To check the property, you can use:
  ```bash
  az webapp show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> --query http20Enabled
  ```
  This command will return `true` if HTTP version 2.0 is enabled, facilitating an automated compliance check.
